description:
You are acting as a database administrator who wants to automate daily extraction of slow SQL statements found in the general‐query log.  
The raw log for today is already present at  

    /home/user/db_logs/queries.log  

and looks like a typical “pipe separated” record:

    YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | <full-query-text>; | <runtime_in_ms>ms

Your goal is to create a *new* summary file that only lists statements that took at least one full second (1000 ms) to finish, sorted from the slowest down to the fastest.

Exact requirements your single command **must** satisfy  

1. It must create a new file named  
       /home/user/db_logs/slow_queries_20240601.log  
   (exactly that name, no variation, overwrite if it exists).  
   • The date part “20240601” is fixed for this exercise; you do not need to compute it dynamically.  

2. The output file must start with one header line **exactly**:  
       # timestamp | exec_time_ms | query  

3. Every following data line must have this layout, **including a literal single space on both sides of each pipe (“|”) character**:

       YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | <exec_time_without_ms_suffix> | <query_text>;

   Example of a valid line (spaces and semicolon included):  
       2024-06-01 09:12:14 | 5344 | SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE status='failed';

4. The file must contain **only** statements whose runtime is **≥ 1000 ms** in the raw log.

5. Lines must be sorted in strictly descending numerical order of runtime (so the slowest query appears first, fastest last).

6. Accommodate *error recovery*:  
   • If /home/user/db_logs/queries.log is missing, your command must **not** crash; instead it must print the literal message  
         Error: log file not found.  
     to STDOUT and leave /home/user/db_logs/slow_queries_20240601.log untouched (do not create or modify it).  
   • You may implement this safeguard using any standard shell mechanism (test operators, “||” chains, etc.), but keep everything inside **one** compound command the user can paste at once.

7. Accomplish everything (filtering, sorting, re-formatting, writing the new file, the fallback error message) in **one single shell command** that the user would run from any directory.

Do not show intermediate steps or explanations—only the compound terminal command itself—so it can be copied and executed verbatim.
truth:
Initial files/directories pre-created for the test:

1. Directory  
   /home/user/db_logs/  
   with default 755 permissions.

2. File  
   /home/user/db_logs/queries.log  
   with 644 permissions and these eight lines (trailing spaces none, each line terminated by a single LF):

2024-06-01 09:12:11 | SELECT * FROM users; | 234ms
2024-06-01 09:12:12 | SELECT * FROM orders WHERE price > 100; | 1245ms
2024-06-01 09:12:13 | UPDATE users SET name='Alice' WHERE id=5; | 67ms
2024-06-01 09:12:14 | SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE status='failed'; | 5344ms
2024-06-01 09:12:15 | DELETE FROM sessions WHERE last_active < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day'; | 908ms
2024-06-01 09:12:16 | SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users; | 321ms
2024-06-01 09:12:17 | INSERT INTO logs(message) VALUES('test'); | 45ms
2024-06-01 09:12:18 | SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status='pending'; | 1023ms

Expected artefact after the agent’s command (if queries.log exists):

File: /home/user/db_logs/slow_queries_20240601.log  
Permissions: 644  
Content (exactly, including header and line order):

# timestamp | exec_time_ms | query
2024-06-01 09:12:14 | 5344 | SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE status='failed';
2024-06-01 09:12:12 | 1245 | SELECT * FROM orders WHERE price > 100;
2024-06-01 09:12:18 | 1023 | SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status='pending';

If /home/user/db_logs/queries.log is removed before running the task, then:

• /home/user/db_logs/slow_queries_20240601.log must *not* be created or modified.  
• The command’s STDOUT must contain exactly:  
    Error: log file not found.

Test harness actions:

1. Verify the new file exists and matches the expected three data lines and header when queries.log is present.  
2. Remove queries.log, run the command again, verify error message appears and the slow_queries_20240601.log timestamp does not change.
